Designated marksman rifle

The OTs-03 **SVU** (ОЦ-03 СВУ, [Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Снайперская винтовка укороченная, **Snájperskaja Vintóvka Ukoróčennaja**, Sniper Rifle Shortened) is a [bullpup](/wiki/Bullpup "Bullpup") [designated marksman rifle](/wiki/Designated_marksman_rifle "Designated marksman rifle"). The SVU was developed to meet the needs of the security forces of the [Russian Ministry of Internal Affairs](/wiki/Russian_Ministry_of_Internal_Affairs "Russian Ministry of Internal Affairs"), such as [OMON](/wiki/OMON "OMON"). The SVU was first seen in use in the [First Chechen War](/wiki/First_Chechen_War "First Chechen War"). Originally, the plan was to just slightly modernize the aging [SVD](/wiki/Dragunov_sniper_rifle "Dragunov sniper rifle"), but the designers eventually realised that the configuration of the weapon would have to be completely altered, leading to the creation of the SVU.

A special muzzle brake was added that could absorb up to 40% of the recoil energy and an elastic butt stock with lamellar spring was non-rigidly attached to the receiver. The acoustics of the rifle were also improved by adding a sound suppressor. The other main improvements made to the SVU over the SVD include replacement of the butt stock, pistol grip, trigger and the mounting for the sight; and shortening the barrel by 100 mm (3.9 in) achieved perfect balance for the weapon.

Around 1991, the Russians developed a slight variant of the OTs-03, the OTs-03A (SVU-A). Whereas the SVU is [semi-automatic](/wiki/Semi-automatic_firearm "Semi-automatic firearm"), the SVU-A (the A stands for automatic) is a [fully automatic](/wiki/Automatic_firearm "Automatic firearm"). In this rifle, the center of gravity was moved forward by the addition of a forward extended bipod mounted on the receiver. Developing 20 and 30 round box magazines increased the capacity of the magazine so that it could be used effectively in full automatic mode; and better optics were incorporated into the SVU-A to replace the previous units, which had been left almost untouched since the SVD.

Though it has folding [iron sights](/wiki/Iron_sights "Iron sights"), the SVU is almost always used with the [PSO-1](/wiki/PSO-1 "PSO-1") scope with illuminated reticle, but other Russian scopes can also be mounted. The PSO-1 reticle is somewhat unique in the world of sniper scopes, in that its rangefinders are in the lower left, chevrons for bullet drop compensation are found in the middle, and stadia marks for windage to the left and right of the center reticule. The reticle is also illuminated by radioactive [tritium](/wiki/Tritium "Tritium") instead of a small battery-powered lamp. The SVU also comes with an adjustable bipod. It has an integral flash hider and [muzzle brake](/wiki/Muzzle_brake "Muzzle brake"). A bayonet may be fitted under the forestock.

## Variants

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dragunov_SVU&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Variants")]

The OTs-03A (SVU-A) (Russian: Снайперская винтовка укороченная, автоматическая, *Snayperskaya Vintovka Ukorochennaya—Avtomaticheskaya*, Short Sniper Rifle—Automatic) is a [selective-fire](/wiki/Selective-fire "Selective-fire") version of the SVU, capable of semi-automatic or fully automatic fire.
